title: linaria heterophylla var. pseudosupina
intro: Linaria heterophylla var. pseudosupina, also known as the toadflax or butter and eggs plant, is a flowering plant species in the figwort family that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Linaria heterophylla var. pseudosupina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support respiratory health. The plant can be prepared as a tea or used in tincture form to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
